## Utilities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Victoria_(state)&action=edit&section=38 "Edit section: Utilities")]

### Energy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Victoria_(state)&action=edit&section=39 "Edit section: Energy")]

Victoria's major utilities include a collection of brown-coal-fired power stations, particularly in the Latrobe Valley. One of these was the recently decommissioned [Hazelwood Power Station](/wiki/Hazelwood_Power_Station "Hazelwood Power Station"), which was number 1 on the worldwide [List of least carbon efficient power stations](/wiki/List_of_least_carbon_efficient_power_stations "List of least carbon efficient power stations"). The Victorian government is aiming to cut 40.6 megatonnes of greenhouse gas emissions by 2025.[[99]](#cite_note-104)[[100]](#cite_note-105)

### Water

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Victoria_(state)&action=edit&section=40 "Edit section: Water")]

See also: [Water management in Victoria](/wiki/Water_management_in_Victoria "Water management in Victoria")

Victoria's water infrastructure includes a series of dams and reservoirs, predominantly in Central Victoria, that hold and collect water for much of the state. The water collected is of a very high quality and requires little chlorination treatment, giving the water a taste more like water collected in a rainwater tank. In regional areas however, such as in the west of the state, chlorination levels are much higher. The Victorian Water Grid consists of a number of new connections and pipelines being built across the State. This allows water to be moved around Victoria to where it is needed most and reduces the impact of localised droughts in an era thought to be influenced by climate change. Major projects already completed as part of the Grid include the Wimmera Mallee Pipeline and the Goldfields Superpipe.[[101]](#cite_note-106)

## Sport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Victoria_(state)&action=edit&section=41 "Edit section: Sport")]

Main article: [Sport in Victoria](/wiki/Sport_in_Victoria "Sport in Victoria")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f0/Tom_Wills_Statue.jpg/250px-Tom_Wills_Statue.jpg)](/wiki/File:Tom_Wills_Statue.jpg)

Statue outside the [Melbourne Cricket Ground](/wiki/Melbourne_Cricket_Ground "Melbourne Cricket Ground") commemorating the origins of [Australian rules football](/wiki/Australian_rules_football "Australian rules football")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/42/2017_AFL_Grand_Final_panorama_during_national_anthem.jpg/330px-2017_AFL_Grand_Final_panorama_during_national_anthem.jpg)](/wiki/File:2017_AFL_Grand_Final_panorama_during_national_anthem.jpg)

Panorama of the [MCG](/wiki/Melbourne_Cricket_Ground "Melbourne Cricket Ground") during the AFL Grand Final on 30 September 2017

Victoria is the home of [Australian rules football](/wiki/Australian_rules_football "Australian rules football"), with ten of the 18 [Australian Football League](/wiki/Australian_Football_League "Australian Football League") (AFL) clubs based in the state. The [AFL Grand Final](/wiki/AFL_Grand_Final "AFL Grand Final") is traditionally held at the [Melbourne Cricket Ground](/wiki/Melbourne_Cricket_Ground "Melbourne Cricket Ground") on the last Saturday of September. The state has a public holiday the day before the Grand Final, which coincides with the AFL Grand Final parade. The MCG is sometimes called the spiritual home of Australian rules football.[[102]](#cite_note-107)

The [Victorian](/wiki/Victoria_cricket_team "Victoria cricket team") cricket team play in the national [Sheffield Shield](/wiki/Sheffield_Shield "Sheffield Shield") cricket competition. Victoria is represented in the [National Rugby League](/wiki/National_Rugby_League "National Rugby League") by the [Melbourne Storm](/wiki/Melbourne_Storm "Melbourne Storm"). Prior to their axing at the end of the [2024 season](/wiki/2024_Super_Rugby_Pacific_season "2024 Super Rugby Pacific season"), Victoria was represented by the [Melbourne Rebels](/wiki/Melbourne_Rebels "Melbourne Rebels") in the [Super Rugby](/wiki/Super_Rugby "Super Rugby"). It is represented in the [National Basketball League](/wiki/National_Basketball_League_(Australia) "National Basketball League (Australia)") by [Melbourne United](/wiki/Melbourne_United "Melbourne United") and [South East Melbourne Phoenix](/wiki/South_East_Melbourne_Phoenix "South East Melbourne Phoenix"). It is also represented in [soccer](/wiki/Soccer "Soccer") by [Melbourne Victory](/wiki/Melbourne_Victory "Melbourne Victory"), [Melbourne City](/wiki/Melbourne_City_FC "Melbourne City FC") and [Western United](/wiki/Western_United_FC "Western United FC") in the [A-League](/wiki/A-League "A-League"). Melbourne has held the [1956 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1956_Summer_Olympics "1956 Summer Olympics"), [2006 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/2006_Commonwealth_Games "2006 Commonwealth Games") and the FINA World Swimming Championship.

Melbourne is also home to the [Australian Open](/wiki/Australian_Open "Australian Open") tennis tournament in January each year, which is the first of the world's four Grand Slam tennis tournaments, as well as the Formula One [Australian Grand Prix](/wiki/Australian_Grand_Prix "Australian Grand Prix"), which is, on an annual basis, usually held in March or April. It hosted the [Australian Masters](/wiki/Australian_Masters "Australian Masters") golf tournament from 1979 to 2015. Victoria's Bells Beach hosts one of the world's longest-running surfing competition, the Bells Beach SurfClassic, which is part of The [ASP World Tour](/wiki/ASP_World_Tour "ASP World Tour"). The [Melbourne Vixens](/wiki/Melbourne_Vixens "Melbourne Vixens") and [Melbourne Mavericks](/wiki/Melbourne_Mavericks "Melbourne Mavericks") represent Victoria in the [Super Netball](/wiki/Super_Netball "Super Netball") league.

Victoria's [Phillip Island](/wiki/Phillip_Island_(Victoria) "Phillip Island (Victoria)") is home of the [Phillip Island Grand Prix Circuit](/wiki/Phillip_Island_Grand_Prix_Circuit "Phillip Island Grand Prix Circuit") which hosts the [Australian motorcycle Grand Prix](/wiki/Australian_motorcycle_Grand_Prix "Australian motorcycle Grand Prix") which features [MotoGP](/wiki/Grand_Prix_motorcycle_racing "Grand Prix motorcycle racing") (the world's premier motorcycling class), as well as the Australian round of the [World Superbike Championship](/wiki/World_Superbike_Championship "World Superbike Championship") and the domestic [V8 Supercar](/wiki/V8_Supercar "V8 Supercar") racing, which also visits [Sandown Raceway](/wiki/Sandown_Raceway "Sandown Raceway") and the rural [Winton Motor Raceway](/wiki/Winton_Motor_Raceway "Winton Motor Raceway") circuit. Australia's most prestigious footrace, the [Stawell Gift](/wiki/Stawell_Gift "Stawell Gift"), is an annual event. Victoria is also home to the [Aussie Millions](/wiki/Aussie_Millions "Aussie Millions") poker tournament, the tournament with the highest potential proceeds in the Southern Hemisphere.

The main horse racing tracks in Victoria are [Caulfield Racecourse](/wiki/Caulfield_Racecourse "Caulfield Racecourse"), [Flemington Racecourse](/wiki/Flemington_Racecourse "Flemington Racecourse") and [Sandown Racecourse](/wiki/Sandown_Racecourse "Sandown Racecourse"). The [Melbourne Spring Racing Carnival](/wiki/Melbourne_Spring_Racing_Carnival "Melbourne Spring Racing Carnival") is one of the biggest horse racing events in the world and is one of the world's largest sporting events. The main race is for the $6 million [Melbourne Cup](/wiki/Melbourne_Cup "Melbourne Cup"), and crowds for the carnival usually exceed 700,000. Victoria was due to host the [2026 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/2026_Commonwealth_Games "2026 Commonwealth Games") but withdrew on 18 July 2023 as a result of increased costs of holding the event.[[103]](#cite_note-108)[[104]](#cite_note-109)

Major professional teams include:

* [Australian rules football](/wiki/Australian_rules_football "Australian rules football") ([AFL](/wiki/Australian_Football_League "Australian Football League")): [Carlton](/wiki/Carlton_Football_Club "Carlton Football Club"), [Collingwood](/wiki/Collingwood_Football_Club "Collingwood Football Club"), [Essendon](/wiki/Essendon_Football_Club "Essendon Football Club"), [Geelong Cats](/wiki/Geelong_Football_Club "Geelong Football Club"), [Hawthorn](/wiki/Hawthorn_Football_Club "Hawthorn Football Club"), [Melbourne](/wiki/Melbourne_Football_Club "Melbourne Football Club"), [North Melbourne](/wiki/North_Melbourne_Football_Club "North Melbourne Football Club"), [Richmond](/wiki/Richmond_Football_Club "Richmond Football Club"), [St Kilda](/wiki/St_Kilda_Football_Club "St Kilda Football Club"), [Western Bulldogs](/wiki/Western_Bulldogs "Western Bulldogs")
* [Basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") ([NBL](/wiki/National_Basketball_League_(Australia) "National Basketball League (Australia)")): [Melbourne United](/wiki/Melbourne_United "Melbourne United"), [South East Melbourne Phoenix](/wiki/South_East_Melbourne_Phoenix "South East Melbourne Phoenix")
* [Cricket](/wiki/Cricket "Cricket") ([BBL](/wiki/Big_Bash_League "Big Bash League")): [Melbourne Renegades](/wiki/Melbourne_Renegades "Melbourne Renegades"), [Melbourne Stars](/wiki/Melbourne_Stars "Melbourne Stars")

## Sister states
